Newest Records

Newest folklore, legend, and mystery stories

The latest Kyunolab Mystery Archive entries, including urban legends, internet folklore, myths, strange places, and source-aware mystery notes.

Myths

Persephone and the Underworld: The Greek Myth Behind Loss, Return, and the Seasons

The Persephone myth tells how Persephone is taken to the underworld, how Demeter grieves, and how Persephone returns for part of the year. The cycle is often connected with seasonal change.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Modern Legends

Dover Demon Legend: The Brief Massachusetts Sightings That Became a Modern Monster

The Dover Demon is a modern monster legend based on reports from Dover, Massachusetts, in 1977. Witnesses described a small, strange humanoid figure with an unusual head and glowing eyes.

Modern Legends - Modern Monster Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Classic Folklore

Tomte and Nisse Folklore: The Small Household Guardian With a Sharp Temper

The tomte or nisse is a Nordic household spirit often linked with farms, barns, and family protection. It may help when treated well, but punish disrespect or neglect.

Classic Folklore - Household Spirit - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Unexplained Mysteries

Amber Room Mystery: The Lost Treasure That Still Refuses a Final Ending

The Amber Room was an ornate chamber of amber panels and gold decoration that disappeared during World War II. A reconstruction exists, but the fate of the original remains disputed.

Unexplained Mysteries - Lost Treasure Mystery - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Strange Places

Eastern State Penitentiary Haunting: Why an Abandoned Prison Became a Strange Place

Eastern State Penitentiary is a former prison in Philadelphia whose architecture, solitary-confinement history, and abandoned appearance helped create a strong haunting reputation.

Strange Places - Haunted Prison - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Internet Folklore

The Rake Creepypasta: How a Pale Figure Became Internet Folklore

The Rake is an internet horror figure usually described as a pale, unnatural humanoid creature. It spread through creepypasta-style posts, retellings, images, and fragmented witness-like accounts.

Internet Folklore - Creepypasta Creature - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Urban Legends

Resurrection Mary Legend: The Chicago Hitchhiker Who Keeps Returning to the Road

Resurrection Mary is a Chicago-area ghost legend about a young woman who appears near Archer Avenue, accepts or requests a ride, and vanishes near Resurrection Cemetery.

Urban Legends - Vanishing Hitchhiker - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Legend Origins

Horseshoe Luck Origin: Iron, Doorways, and How a Stable Object Became a Protective Sign

Horseshoe luck is a superstition that treats the horseshoe as a protective or lucky object, often placed above a doorway. Explanations usually involve iron, horses, blacksmithing, shape, and the boundary of the home.

Legend Origins - Luck Symbol Origin - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Mythic Objects

Ark of the Covenant Legend: Sacred Chest, Lost Relic, and the Object No One Can Treat as Ordinary

The Ark of the Covenant is the sacred chest described in biblical tradition as connected with the tablets of the covenant and divine presence. Later legend focuses on its power, disappearance, and possible hidden location.

Mythic Objects - Sacred Object Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Legendary Places

Stonehenge Legend: Ancient Stones, Merlin Stories, and the Monument That Still Refuses One Meaning

Stonehenge is a prehistoric stone monument in Wiltshire that has gathered archaeological interpretation, medieval legend, and modern sacred-place meanings. It is real, but no single explanation contains its full cultural afterlife.

Legendary Places - Legendary Monument - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Strange Nature

Blood Rain Phenomenon: Red Rain, Omen Stories, and the Sky That Falls in the Wrong Color

Blood rain refers to rain that appears red or reddish because particles such as dust, sand, spores, or other material color the water. In many periods, red rain also became an omen story because the sky seemed to be raining blood.

Strange Nature - Strange Weather - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Lost Worlds

Atlantis Lost City: Plato’s Island, Sunken Power, and the World That Vanished Beneath the Sea

Atlantis is the legendary island power described by Plato and later imagined as a lost civilization that sank beneath the sea. The original account appears in philosophical dialogue, not as a modern archaeological report.

Lost Worlds - Lost City Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
